name: Manas Kaul
description: I am a Software Development Engineer II specializing in architecting distributed event-driven systems, microservices, and high-availability cloud infrastructure. With deep expertise across Java, Spring Boot, and Kafka, I thrive on building resilient, scalable platforms that process millions of events daily.

projects.name: Golden Copy Data Source
description: Architected and implemented a distributed event processing platform (PostgreSQL CDC → Kafka → stateless consumer microservices) processing 1M+ events/day with <1s end-to-end delivery SLA.

Designed authoritative event-sourcing model backed by PostgreSQL (ACID-compliant) with Kafka-based propagation, eliminating dependency on external caching layers and ensuring strong consistency under retries and failures.
Led schema design for 500+ attributes per event, introducing versioned schema strategy and extensible data modeling to support high-throughput OLTP writes.
Built a real-time rule evaluation engine supporting thousands of user-defined filters with <150ms matching latency, enabling personalized event delivery at scale.
Authored low-level design (LLD) and drove implementation across cross-functional stakeholders (data, infra, consumer teams), defining API contracts adopted by downstream services.
Owned performance tuning and capacity planning, benchmarking throughput (~700 events/min sustained) and identifying bottlenecks across database writes and consumer lag.

projects.name: Electronic Affirmation Router
description: Designed and implemented multi-venue trade routing flow across 10+ microservices deployed on Kubernetes in JPMC private cloud, improving routing latency by 40% across complex trade setups.
Contributed to architecture handling ~1000 trades/day with daily notional value exceeding $200M, ensuring high reliability and accurate downstream settlement workflows.
Improved observability by standardizing structured logging, health checks, and alerting pipelines across services, reducing incident response time by 50% and improving SLA adherence.

projects.name: Equipment Licensing and Location Management (ELLM)
description: Owned production stability, CI/CD pipelines, and release coordination, ensuring smooth rollout and post-launch support.
Led end-to-end design and delivery for a 3-engineer team, driving architecture decisions and production rollout adopted across multiple regions.
Architected backend services using Spring Boot with REST APIs and relational data modeling, supporting regulatory workflows that contributed to $20M+ annual revenue enablement.
Led migration from legacy on-prem infrastructure to cloud-native deployment, decommissioning legacy systems and reducing operational costs by $50K+ annually.
Introduced Micro-Frontend architecture using Module Federation, enabling independent deployments and reducing release cycle time by 30%, improving parallel team productivity.

subsection.heading: DA-IICT, Gujarat
description: Completed rigorous coursework with a strong focus on data structures, distributed systems, and modern software engineering architecture.
